items.builder_takeaway: Next.js canary release upgrading to rspack 2.0 and adding experimental React compiler support for Turbopack. Pre-release only; rspack bump is maintenance-level, Turbopack React compiler is experimental.
source_facts: Next.js v16.3.0-canary.52 upgrades next-rspack to rspack 2.0 and adds experimental React compiler support for Turbopack.
items.title: Next.js v16.3.0-canary.52
items.uncertainty: Canary builds are unstable; changes may not land in stable as described.

items.builder_takeaway: Vercel Workflow SDK 5 beta adds AbortController/AbortSignal support across workflow and step boundaries, allowing cancellation of in-flight operations that survive suspensions and deterministic replay. Beta, Vercel-specific.
source_facts: Vercel Workflow SDK 5 beta now supports AbortController/AbortSignal for cancelling in-flight workflow steps across suspension boundaries.
items.title: Workflow SDK now supports inflight cancellation
items.uncertainty: Beta product; API may change before stable release.

items.builder_takeaway: Vercel Workflow SDK now supports TanStack Start apps on Vercel using the existing Vite/Nitro plugin. Niche: requires both TanStack Start and the Vercel platform.
source_facts: Vercel Workflow SDK now supports TanStack Start applications with durable, resumable workflow functions via the workflow/vite plugin.
items.title: Workflow SDK now supports TanStack Start
items.uncertainty: TanStack Start itself is still relatively new.

items.builder_takeaway: Changes the calculus for long-running tasks on Vercel — previously you'd need a queue/worker setup to stay under the old limit.
source_facts: Vercel Pro/Enterprise Functions can now run up to 30 minutes for Node.js and Python runtimes, up from the previous 800-second limit.
items.title: Vercel Functions can now run up to 30 minutes
items.uncertainty: Pro/Enterprise only; other runtimes not yet supported.

items.builder_takeaway: A latent.space AI newsletter issue covering Satya Nadella's 'Loopcraft' essay about building frontier AI ecosystems. Editorial commentary on a quiet news day; no actionable claim.
source_facts: AI News newsletter covers Satya Nadella's Loopcraft essay about building frontier AI ecosystems.
items.title: [AINews] Satya on Loopcraft: Building Frontier Ecosystems
items.uncertainty: Secondary source; unclear what the essay argues without reading it.

items.builder_takeaway: Simon Willison quotes Kate Moussouris (Luta Security) on the Fable 5 jailbreak situation: the White House classified Claude as a potential weapons-enabling tool, Anthropic is pushing back. Policy commentary with no independent actionable claim beyond the main model-offline story.
source_facts: Cybersecurity expert Kate Moussouris argues that applying export controls to AI models due to the Fable 5 jailbreak harms US cyber defense capabilities.
items.title: The Fable 5 Export Controls Harm US Cyber Defense
items.uncertainty: Policy situation is evolving; outcome unclear.

items.builder_takeaway: Simon Willison quotes The Atlantic's Matteo Wong on the Fable jailbreak: cybersecurity expert Moussouris confirmed the jailbreak was genuine but argues export controls are the wrong response. Duplicate angle on the main Anthropic outage story.
source_facts: Katie Moussouris confirmed the Fable jailbreak of Claude was real but argues that applying export controls as a result harms US cyber defense.
items.title: Anthropic/Claude and the Fable 5 export control situation — The Atlantic
items.uncertainty: Attribution via anonymous sources; Anthropic not commenting publicly.

items.builder_takeaway: Practical gotcha that would be hard to debug without knowing the cause — saves hours of debugging time.
source_facts: Cloudflare WAF Managed Challenge rules can trigger CAPTCHA on URLs containing literal ampersands, breaking faceted search engines and similar query-string-heavy pages.
items.title: Cloudflare CAPTCHA triggered by URLs containing ampersands
items.uncertainty: May depend on specific WAF rule configuration; not necessarily universal across all Cloudflare setups.

items.builder_takeaway: Human approval pattern for agent DB writes is directly applicable to MCP server design for agentic workflows.
source_facts: datasette-agent 0.3a0 adds execute_write_sql tool that requests user approval before writing to the database, implementing human-in-the-loop for agent DB writes.
items.title: datasette-agent 0.3a0: execute_write_sql with user approval

items.builder_takeaway: Direct impact on Claude API availability — affects any builder running Claude-dependent automation or agents.
source_facts: Anthropic's Claude models were taken offline due to conflict with the US government over the Fable 5 jailbreak and export control classification.
items.title: Anthropic's Claude models went offline due to US government policy clash over Fable jailbreak
items.uncertainty: Based on anonymous sources cited in Axios; Anthropic has not made official statements. Exact timeline and restoration status unclear.

items.builder_takeaway: Raymond Chen (Old New Thing) recounts a story from Microsoft's x86-on-ARM emulation team finding application code so broken they implemented runtime patches in the emulator itself. Historical tech story with no actionable implications for current projects.
source_facts: Microsoft's Windows-on-ARM x86 emulation team implemented runtime patches for application code too broken to run without fixes during emulation.
items.title: The time the x86 emulator team found code so bad they fixed it during emulation

items.builder_takeaway: A link to a John Carmack tweet expressing admiration for Fabrice Bellard. No substantive content beyond a social media post.
source_facts: John Carmack posted on X expressing admiration for Fabrice Bellard.
items.title: John Carmack on Fabrice Bellard
items.uncertainty: Tweet content not available without accessing X.

items.builder_takeaway: Real documented attack vector targeting developers via LinkedIn — high risk for solo devs who review code for freelance or contract work.
source_facts: A security researcher documented a supply chain attack where a LinkedIn job offer directed developers to clone a GitHub repo that installed a persistent backdoor.
items.title: A backdoor in a LinkedIn job offer
items.uncertainty: Specifics of the backdoor mechanism depend on reading the full post.

items.builder_takeaway: A creative project embedding a library of banned books in a smart light bulb's WiFi captive portal. Clever hardware hack but no practical application for solo builder workflows.
source_facts: A developer embedded a banned book library accessible via captive WiFi portal in a smart light bulb.
items.title: Banned Book Library in a Wi-Fi Smart Light Bulb

items.builder_takeaway: Stable 1.0 P2P library for direct device-to-device connections without centralized infrastructure.
source_facts: Iroh 1.0 released — a stable Rust P2P networking library that uses public keys instead of IPs for connections, with NAT traversal and relay fallback.
items.title: Iroh 1.0: Dial Keys, not IPs
items.uncertainty: Rust-only for now; ecosystem adoption unclear.

items.builder_takeaway: A historical archive of text-based visual art predating ASCII art, created from a 2015 BA thesis on Amiga ASCII art. Interesting historical artifact; not actionable for solo builder work.
source_facts: A developer published an archive of pre-ASCII pictorial typography based on their 2015 BA thesis research on Amiga ASCII art.
items.title: Garden of Flowers: archive of pictorial typography before ASCII art

items.builder_takeaway: An experience report of running FreeBSD 15 as a laptop daily driver. Not relevant to Fuzzy's macOS/Linux/Hetzner stack.
source_facts: A developer published their experience running FreeBSD 15 as a laptop daily driver.
items.title: FreeBSD 15 on a Laptop

items.builder_takeaway: Hacker News discussion thread for the Iroh 1.0 release blog post. Same underlying story as item 016532f2; no additional content warranting a separate entry.
source_facts: Hacker News discussion of the Iroh 1.0 P2P networking library release.
items.title: Iroh 1.0 — Hacker News discussion

items.builder_takeaway: Notable release for document generation tooling; worth checking for PDF/report generation workflows.
source_facts: Typst 0.15 released with significant new features in the modern LaTeX-alternative document typesetting system.
items.title: Typst 0.15 released
items.uncertainty: HN link is to comments; full feature list requires reading the official blog post.
